frame_analyses.description: Extracorporeal knot formation being demonstrated with red suture using hands
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean knot formation technique shown, appears to be surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: LapPass trainer setup visible with Penrose drain positioned between blue marks
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper setup with drain positioned for suturing task

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle and positioning for entry at first mark
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle positioned at appropriate angle for entry

frame_analyses.description: Needle being driven through drain at first mark location
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry appears close to target mark

frame_analyses.description: Needle being positioned for exit at second mark
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper technique with controlled needle passage

frame_analyses.description: Suture ends being prepared for extracorporeal knot tying
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both suture ends visible and accessible for knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher being used to advance tied knot down to tissue level
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper knot pusher technique with controlled advancement

frame_analyses.description: Scissors cutting suture ends after knot placement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cutting technique with appropriate tail length

penalties.description: Entry point appears approximately 1-2mm from first mark

confidence_rationale: Camera angle makes precise measurement of suture deviation challenging. Knot quality and completion well-observed. Total time estimated from visible phases.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation measurements due to camera perspective
technique_summary: Good intermediate performance with competent extracorporeal knot technique. Time within intermediate range with minimal deviation penalties.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise needle entry at marks to reduce deviation
Practice to reduce suture placement time from 44s to under 30s
